scenario.description: A national supermarket chain plans to reconfigure shelf space allocations for key consumer staples categories, but must align with its largest branded supplier to balance category profitability with brand visibility and supply constraints. Neither the retailer’s merchandising team nor the supplier’s trade marketing team can finalize the new planogram without reconciling sales data, inventory limitations, and promotional commitments from both sides.

goal.content: Create a single joint planogram file that specifies the agreed shelf layout for the top 50 consumer staples SKUs in the retailer’s central region stores.
requirements: Include a master planogram metadata section specifying region (Central), category scope (grocery staples), version number, effective start and end dates, creation timestamp, and names/roles of both collaborating parties (FreshLane category manager and PrimeHarvest trade marketing lead).

Include a ranked SKU list table for the top 50 consumer staples SKUs, where each row contains: SKU ID, UPC, brand, sub-brand, product description, pack size, flavor/variant, unit size (e.g., oz, lbs), case pack, product category, subcategory, and total Central Region sales rank based on agreed period.

Include a shelf layout specification for each shelf (shelf ID/level) and bay in the standard Central Region store format, where each record contains: bay ID, shelf level (e.g., 1–5 from floor), linear feet allocated, shelf height, and orientation rules (e.g., vertical brand block, horizontal size block).

Include a SKU placement mapping where each SKU appears exactly once per planogram, and each record specifies: bay ID, shelf level, horizontal position start (inches from bay left), number of facings, vertical facings (if stacked), maximum shelf quantity, minimum shelf quantity (for replenishment trigger), and adjacency constraints (e.g., must sit next to brand family or competitor set).

Include a constraint and agreement notes section listing, for each of the 50 SKUs: agreed minimum and maximum facings, minimum weekly supply capacity from PrimeHarvest, inventory risk flag (e.g., constrained/normal), promotional commitments (on/off promo, promo dates, expected lift factor), and any special merchandising rules (e.g., eye-level placement, end-cap exclusion, no top shelf).

profile.organization: FreshLane Supermarkets – Central Region Merchandising Department
profile.expertise: Category manager for grocery staples, responsible for optimizing in-store assortment, shelf allocation, and margin performance based on store-level sales data, shopper behavior insights, and corporate merchandising policies.
